methods.name: pulsar.client.openai_like.OpenAiApiLike.chat_completion
documentation:
Generate chat completion using Groq's API

Args:
    messages: List of Message objects containing the conversation
    model: The model ID
    system: Optional system message to set context
    response_type: Return type (str for content only, dict for full response)
    stream: Whether to stream the response
    temperature: Sampling temperature (0-2)
    max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate
    use_cot: Inject CoT prompt at end
    prompt_template: Use custom jinja2 prompt template
    **kwargs: Additional parameters to pass to the API

Returns:
    Generated response as specified by response_type

modules.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer
documentation: json_tokenizer.py

classes.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenType

class_variables.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenType.BOOLEAN
documentation: true, false

class_variables.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenType.NULL
documentation: null

class_variables.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenType.IDENTIFIER
documentation: unquoted string

class_variables.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenType.WHITESPACE
documentation: spaces, tabs, newlines

class_variables.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenType.EOF
documentation: end of input

Enumeration of possible JSON token types.

Includes all standard JSON tokens plus additional types for preprocessing.

classes.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.Token
documentation:
A class representing a token in the JSON text.

Attributes:
    type (TokenType): The type of token
    value (str): The actual text value of the token
    start (int): Starting position in source text
    end (int): Ending position in source text
    depth (int): Nesting depth of the token

classes.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.JSONTokenizer

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.JSONTokenizer.tokenize
documentation:
Convert input text into a list of tokens.

Args:
    text (str): Input JSON text

Returns:
    List[Token]: List of tokens representing the JSON structure

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.JSONTokenizer._next_token
documentation:
Get the next token from the input text.

Returns:
    Token: The next token in the sequence

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.JSONTokenizer._is_type_signature_context
documentation:
Check if current context is within a type signature.

Returns:
    bool: True if currently parsing a type signature

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.JSONTokenizer._consume_whitespace
documentation:
Consume and return whitespace characters as a token.

Returns:
    Token: A whitespace token

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.JSONTokenizer._consume_string
documentation:
Consume and return a string literal as a token.

Handles single, double, and triple quoted strings.

Returns:
    Token: A string token

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.JSONTokenizer._consume_number
documentation:
Consume and return a numeric literal as a token.

Handles integers, floats, and numbers with commas.

Returns:
    Token: A number token

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.JSONTokenizer._consume_identifier
documentation:
Consume and return an identifier as a token.

Handles unquoted strings and keywords.

Returns:
    Token: An identifier token

A tokenizer that converts JSON text into a sequence of tokens.

Handles standard JSON syntax plus additional features like:
- Unquoted strings
- Comments
- Special characters
- Nested structures

classes.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenStream

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenStream.peek
documentation:
Look at the next token without consuming it.

Returns:
    Token: The next token in the stream

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenStream.next
documentation:
Get the next token and advance position.

Returns:
    Token: The next token in the stream

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenStream.backup
documentation: Move back one token in the stream.

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_tokenizer.TokenStream.has_more
documentation:
Check if there are more tokens in the stream.

Returns:
    bool: True if more tokens are available

A stream interface for working with a sequence of tokens.

Provides methods for traversing and inspecting tokens.

modules.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor
documentation: json_preprocessor.py

classes.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.Candidate
documentation:
A class representing a potential JSON data candidate with an associated score.

Attributes:
    text (str): The text content of the candidate
    score (float): The candidate's score, indicating its validity as JSON data

classes.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor._build_json_pattern
documentation:
Build a regex pattern for matching balanced JSON objects.

Returns:
    str: A regular expression pattern that matches JSON object structures

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor.extract_content_blocks
documentation:
Extract JSON content blocks from input text, prioritizing JSON structure.

Args:
    text (str): Input text containing one or more JSON blocks

Returns:
    List[str]: List of extracted JSON content blocks

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor._split_json_objects
documentation:
Split a string containing multiple JSON objects into individual objects.

Args:
    text (str): Input text containing multiple JSON objects

Returns:
    List[str]: List of individual JSON object strings

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor.remove_comments
documentation:
Remove comments from JSON text while preserving string literals.

Args:
    text (str): Input text containing potential comments

Returns:
    str: Text with comments removed

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor.strip_surrounding_text
documentation:
Strip non-JSON text before and after JSON content.

Args:
    text (str): Input text potentially containing surrounding non-JSON content

Returns:
    str: Clean JSON content with surrounding text removed

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor.fix_string_escapes
documentation:
Fix string escapes for any triple-quoted strings in JSON.

Args:
    text (str): Input text containing potential string escapes

Returns:
    str: Text with properly escaped strings

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor.score_candidate
documentation:
Score a candidate based on JSON structure validity.

Args:
    text (str): Candidate JSON text to score

Returns:
    float: Score indicating likelihood of valid JSON structure

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor.select_best_candidate
documentation:
Select the best JSON candidate from multiple possibilities.

Args:
    candidates (List[str]): List of potential JSON candidates

Returns:
    str: The best candidate based on scoring

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor._is_numeric
documentation:
Check if text represents a numeric value, including those with commas.

Args:
    text (str): Text to check

Returns:
    bool: True if text represents a valid number

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor._is_primitive_value
documentation:
Check if text represents a JSON primitive value.

Args:
    text (str): Text to check

Returns:
    bool: True if text represents a JSON primitive

methods.name: pulsar.parser.json_preprocessor.JSONPreprocessor.preprocess
documentation:
Main preprocessing function that handles all cleanup steps.

Args:
    text (str): Input text to preprocess
    allow_partial (bool): Whether to allow partial JSON structures

Returns:
    Union[str, List[str]]: Preprocessed JSON text or list of JSON texts

A class for preprocessing and cleaning JSON-like text data before parsing.

The preprocessor handles various tasks including:
- Removing comments
- Extracting JSON content from markdown blocks
- Handling multiple JSON candidates
- Fixing common formatting issues

    depth (int): Nesting depth in the tree
    start (int): Starting position in source
    end (int): Ending position in source
    parent (Optional[ASTNode]): Parent node reference

Parse a JSON value from the token stream.

Parse an object key from the token stream.

Builds an Abstract Syntax Tree from JSON tokens.

Handles parsing of JSON structures and creation of the AST.

functions.name: pulsar.helpers.func_pydc.function_to_json
documentation:
Converts a Python function into a JSON-serializable dictionary
that describes the function's signature, including its name,
description, and parameters.

Args:

Returns:
    A dictionary representing the function's signature in JSON format.

functions.name: pulsar.helpers.func_pydc.greet
documentation:
Greets the user. Make sure to get their name and age before calling.

Args:
    name: Name of the user.
    age: Age of the user.
    location: Best place on earth.

functions.name: pulsar.promptlib.json_schema
documentation:
Convert a type hint or Pydantic model to a JSON schema representation and return its length.

Args:
    from_type: The type to convert. Can be:
        - Primitive types (str, int, float, bool, None)
        - Pydantic models
        - Lists of supported types
        - Unions of supported types
        - Optional types
        - Literal types
        - Enum classes
    use_md: If True, wraps the output in markdown code block
    show_name: If True, shows Pydantic model names in comments

Returns:
    A tuple containing:
    - The JSON schema representation as a string
    - The number of possible types (1 for primitive/models, 2+ for unions/optional)
